result:
Build is green; only warnings.

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:1316` in `str_gsub` used `state.get_at(1).clone()` — an absolute stack index — to re-push the original string when no substitution occurred. `get_at(idx)` indexes the raw stack, not the active call frame, so when `gsub` was called from within another function (e.g. `len(s)` calling `string.gsub`), `stack[1]` happened to be a function value rather than the gsub argument, producing `function` instead of the source string. A sibling bug at line 1218 (the `Function` replacement arm) used the same wrong pattern for arg 3.

**Fix:** Replaced both with frame-relative `state.push_value_at(idx)?` (which mirrors C `lua_pushvalue(L, idx)` and goes through `index2value`). Two-line change in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs`.

**State after fix:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is clean. Running `reference/lua-c/testes/utf8.lua` now advances past the original "attempt to get length of a function value" error; the test now fails later with a different error ("attempt to call a nil value") — a separate bug for the next loop iteration.

tool_input.command:
cat > /tmp/test_utf8.lua <<'EOF'
print("before require")
local utf8 = require'utf8'
print("type of utf8:", type(utf8))
print("type of utf8.charpattern:", type(utf8.charpattern))
print("utf8.charpattern:", utf8.charpattern)
print("type of utf8.offset:", type(utf8.offset))
local r = utf8.offset("alo", 5)
print("type of result:", type(r), r)
EOF
target/debug/lua-rs /tmp/test_utf8.lua 2>&1 | tail -20

tool_input.description: Run first 200 lines of utf8 test
